headline: Microsoft Slips as OpenAI Bet Starts to Worry Investors
summary: Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) stock is sliding approximately 2% to 3% in Tuesday trading, with shares hovering around $374. These are quiet-looking numbers on the surface, but for a company this closely watched, they carry weight. headline: Carnival Corporation to report Q1 earnings amid higher fuel costs, regional mix shifts
summary: Carnival Corp (NYSE:CCL) is set to report its first quarter earnings on March 27, with focus expected to be on fuel costs, near-term demand, and regional pricing trends, according to Bank of America analysts. Carnival will be the first unhedged, commodity-exposed travel company to report...

headline: BofA: Microsoft ’a primary beneficiary of AI monetization’, sees stock upside
summary: Investing.com -- Bank of America reinstated coverage of Microsoft with a Buy rating and a $500 price target in a note on Tuesday, arguing the company sits “at the center of the AI supercycle.”

headline: Dell Stock (DELL) Will Rise Further, Says Five-Star Analyst
summary: Bank of America (BAC) has raised its price target on Dell Technologies (DELL) even though the shares are currently trading near an all-time high. Wamsi Mohan, a top five-star rated analyst, writes in a note to clients that Dell will continue to benefit from “very robust” demand for artificial intelligence (A.I.) servers during an Asia supply-chain review. headline: Oracle Stock Could See 29% Upside According to Bank of America
summary: Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) has had a turbulent stretch heading into this analyst call. Shares are down 24.20% year-to-date and around 55% below their 52-week high of $345.72. Currently trading around $147.94, the stock is still inspiring some hope among the broader analyst community, which carries a consensus target of $249.02, reflecting a more optimistic longer-term view. ... headline: Investors Are Betting on an Interest Rate Hike That’s Really Not That Likely
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Investors have lifted odds that the Federal Reserve is on the verge of raising interest rates, but lingering labor-market fragility and the risks to growth from surging oil prices make that a long shot, at least for now.On March 19, federal funds rate futures showed a 6% chance of an April hike, and have stayed in positive territory since. This marked the first time since December 2023 that investors saw a hike at the central bank’s next policy meeting as more likely than a cut.Th

headline: ‘A giant going all-in on AI infrastructure’: BofA reinstates Oracle at Buy
summary: Investing.com -- Bank of America (BofA) reinstated coverage of Oracle at Buy with a $200 price objective, citing accelerating demand for AI infrastructure and cloud services, alongside a large backlog of long-term contracts. The price target implies roughly 30% upside from current levels.
